name: Circular gravel through Ourense and Castrelo do Miño
name_original: Circular gravel por Ourense y Castrelo do Miño
description:
The region of Castrelo de Miño, cradle of the famous Ribeiro wine, is a fertile and undulating territory dominated by landscapes of extensive vineyards. Surrounded by mountains, it has a special climate that has favored its use and occupation by different peoples throughout history.

This route that I propose starts from the city of Ourense and takes you to visit the Val de Allariz and its villages until you reach Celanova and return, crossing the Arnoia river, to the Castrelo de Miño reservoir. In the last part of the route, you will go up the Miño River along its right bank until you return to the capital.

There are 107 kilometers and just over 1,700 meters of positive elevation gain in a demanding and somewhat leg-breaking route, although without major technical complications. The last 35 kilometers are very bearable; so if you get past the first 70, you'll have done the hardest part and the rest will be enjoying rolling along the riverbank.

The Route begins and ends at the FF station. DC. from Ourense, in case you want to combine public transport to get there.
region_intro: Gravel biking around Ourense offers diverse natural landscapes, characterized by the Miño River and its thermal route, and the dramatic Sil Canyon within the Ribeira Sacra. The region features a mix of gravel and paved sections, with terrain ranging from riverine paths to mountainous areas with significant elevation gains. This geological makeup, with rainwater filtering through granite, contributes to varied riding conditions and unique natural springs. Best gravel bike trails around Ourense The most popular gravel bike trail is Circular gravel through Ourense and Castrelo do Miño, a 66.3 miles (106.7 km) trail that takes 9 hours 27 minutes to complete. This demanding route explores the landscapes around Ourense and Castrelo do Miño. Another top favourite among local gravel bikers is A Boutureira Waterfall loop from A Merca, a moderate 11.7 miles (18.9 km) path. This route leads to the A Boutureira Waterfall, offering a scenic ride through the local countryside. Local gravel bikers also love the Roman Bridge of Ourense – Cachamuíña Reservoir loop from Rabaza de Abaixo, a 74.2 miles (119.4 km) trail leading through river valleys and towards the Cachamuíña Reservoir, often completed in about 11 hours 10 minutes. Gravel biking around Ourense is defined by river routes along the Miño, the dramatic Sil Canyon, and varied terrain that includes both gravel and paved sections. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to challenging long-distance routes. The routes in Ourense are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 30 gravel bikers have used komoot to explore Ourense's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on gravel bike trails around Ourense?
A: Ourense offers a diverse mix of terrain for gravel biking. You'll find routes along the Miño River that feature both gravel and paved sections, generally easy to ride. The Ribeira Sacra and Sil Canyon areas present more challenging terrain with significant elevation gains, deep gorges, and lush forests, ideal for experienced riders. The region's geological makeup, with rainwater filtering through granite, contributes to varied riding conditions.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly gravel routes in Ourense?
A: While many routes in Ourense are rated as difficult, there are options for moderate riders. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23745916" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;A Boutureira Waterfall loop from A Merca&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 18.9 km path with a manageable elevation gain, offering a scenic ride through the countryside.

Q: Can I find long-distance gravel routes in the Ourense region?
A: Yes, Ourense is home to several long-distance gravel routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e825597291/circular-gravel-through-ourense-and-castrelo-do-mino" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circular gravel through Ourense and Castrelo do Miño&lt;/a&gt;, for example, spans over 106 km with significant elevation, making it a demanding ride for those seeking a full-day adventure. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38152229" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Bridge of Ourense – Cachamuíña Reservoir loop from Rabaza de Abaixo&lt;/a&gt;, which covers nearly 120 km.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the gravel routes?
A: Ourense's gravel routes often pass by stunning natural features and historical landmarks. You can encounter thermal springs like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3108574" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Outariz Hot Springs&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2456404" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;As Burgas Roman Baths&lt;/a&gt;, and even waterfalls such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5338813" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barbantiño Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. Many routes also offer views of the Miño River and its historic bridges, or lead towards reservoirs like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3132926" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cachamuíña Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular gravel bike routes available?
A: Absolutely. Many of the gravel routes in Ourense are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e825597291/circular-gravel-through-ourense-and-castrelo-do-mino" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circular gravel through Ourense and Castrelo do Miño&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38152142" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Bridge Ourense – Roman Bridge of Ourense loop from Ourense-San Francisco&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a substantial ride through river valleys.

Q: What is the best time of year for gravel biking in Ourense?
A: Ourense offers good conditions for gravel biking for much of the year. The region's climate allows for pleasant riding in spring and autumn, with milder temperatures. Summer can be warm, especially in the river valleys, but early mornings or late afternoons are often enjoyable. Winter rides are possible, though some higher elevation areas might be cooler. The thermal springs are particularly inviting after a ride in cooler weather.

Q: Can I access gravel trails in Ourense using public transport?
A: Ourense city itself is a hub for public transport, and many routes start directly from or near the city center, making them accessible. While specific public transport options for reaching every trailhead in more remote areas might vary, the city's connectivity allows for a good starting point for many adventures along the Miño River and beyond.

Q: Are there any thermal springs along or near the gravel bike trails for relaxation?
A: Yes, Ourense is famous for its thermal springs, and many are conveniently located along the Miño River, making them perfect for post-ride relaxation. The Thermal Route along the Miño features natural pools and spas, including popular spots like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3108574" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Outariz Hot Springs&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2456404" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;As Burgas Roman Baths&lt;/a&gt;. These offer a unique way to soothe tired muscles after a long ride.

Q: What do other gravel bikers say about the routes in Ourense?
A: The gravel biking experience in Ourense is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Riders often praise the diverse landscapes, from the serene river paths to the dramatic canyons, and the unique opportunity to combine cycling with visits to the region's famous thermal springs.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by waterfalls?
A: Yes, you can incorporate waterfalls into your gravel biking adventures. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23745916" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;A Boutureira Waterfall loop from A Merca&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route specifically designed to lead to the A Boutureira Waterfall. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5338813" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barbantiño Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is another notable natural attraction in the region that can be reached.

Q: What are the main geographical features that define gravel biking in Ourense?
A: Gravel biking in Ourense is primarily defined by the Miño River and its extensive river routes, which offer a mix of flat and rolling terrain. The dramatic Sil Canyon within the Ribeira Sacra is another prominent feature, providing challenging mountainous terrain with deep gorges and stunning viewpoints. This combination of riverine and mountainous landscapes creates a varied and engaging experience for gravel cyclists.

On the shores of the Castrelo de Miño reservoir, rowing teams usually train in this nautical park.
If the weather is good, you can stop to take a refreshing swim.

Decorated in a certain Japanese style, these hot springs are a temptation for cyclists: massages, baths, bar.
The price is affordable and if you have time, it is worth stopping to enjoy its hot waters.
